details.text: Derivative: غِلًّا means bad, malicious or spiteful feelings or resentment.
Derivative: تَنْظُرْ from نَظَرَ which means to watch for a long time and observe. Here it implies to pay special attention.

Derivative: غَدٍ means tomorrow but here it is referring to the future and specifically the Day of Judgement implying how we should consider it in terms of being so close for each and every one of us.

Derivative: خَاشِعًا from خُشُوع which literally means for muscles to become relaxed due to fear. It holds the meaning of the state of being humbled and lowered. Here it means to become humbled as if the mountain’s enormousness and extremely firm solidity just melts away.
Derivative: مُتَصَدِّعًا means to tear, break or split apart i.e., like an implosion or explosion.
Derivative: الْجَبَّارُ – The One who corrects and sets straight with force and might when required.
Derivative: الْمُتَكَبِّرُ – The (real) owner of greatness.
Derivative: الْمُهَيْمِنُ – The One who watches constantly over.
Derivative: السَّلَامُ – The source of all peace.
Derivative: الْمُؤْمِنُ – The One who gives Iman and security.
Derivative: الْقُدُّوسُ– The source of all purity.
Derivative: الْعَزِيزُ – The One who has the ultimate authority.
Derivative: الْمُصَوِّرُ – The moulder and fashioner.
Derivative: الْحَكِيمُ – The owner of all and ultimate wisdom.
Derivative: الْبَارِئُ – The initiator and originator.
Derivative: الْجَلَاءَ means expulsion, departure and evacuation i.e., to be cleared out of an area.
Derivative: شَاقُّوا and يُشَاقِّ from شَاقَّ means to show opposition and animosity. شَقاق means a schism in terms of showing opposition and being at odds with something or someone.

Derivative: أَفَاءَ – يُفيءُ - إِفاءة: فَيْء (Fai’) literally refers to what was left behind and what was returned or came back. In war what the enemy leaves behind on the battlefield in terms of shields, swords and other properties of value, that is called أَنْفال (Anfaal) of غَنِيمَة (Ghaneemah). فَيْء (Fa’i) refers to spoils that are acquired and come as a result of a military expedition without fighting having taken place.

Derivative: يُؤْثِرُونَ from آَثَر – يُؤْثِرُ – إِيثَار which means to give preference to others or to consider them before yourselves. يُؤْثِرُونَ عَلَىٰ أَنْفُسِهِمْ – “They give preference (i.e., to their brothers) over their own selves”.
Derivative: خَصَاصَةٌ means extreme need, poverty and destitution.
Derivative: يُوقَ comes from وَقِيَ – يَقِي which means to protect. يُوقَ is its passive form: To be protected.

- The شُحَّ of the نَفْس literally means the “greed or miserliness of the inner self” refers to that which exists deeply or inherently within people that dislikes giving and wants to hold on to what it has.
